channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Amphibian Conservation Fund 2026 Grant Call Now Open | Funding Available for Global Amphibian Conservation Projects
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Amphibian Conservation Fund, managed by Stiftung Artenschutz (Species Conservation Foundation), is now accepting applications for its 2026 Open Grant Call. The fund supports impactful projects dedicated to protecting threatened amphibian species worldwide through conservation, research, habitat restoration, conservation breeding, education, and capacity-building initiatives. Created in response to the alarming global decline of amphibian populations, […]

The post Amphibian Conservation Fund 2026 Grant Call Now Open | Funding Available for Global Amphibian Conservation Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Amphibian Conservation Fund, managed by Stiftung Artenschutz (Species Conservation Foundation), is now accepting applications for its 2026 Open Grant Call. The fund supports impactful projects dedicated to protecting threatened amphibian species worldwide through conservation, research, habitat restoration, conservation breeding, education, and capacity-building initiatives.

Created in response to the alarming global decline of amphibian populations, the Amphibian Conservation Fund brings together zoos, conservation organizations, and wildlife associations from the German-speaking region to invest in projects that deliver measurable conservation outcomes for some of the world’s most threatened amphibian species.

Organizations and researchers working to safeguard amphibians are encouraged to submit their proposals before the application deadline.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 31 July 2026 (23:59 CET / UTC+2)

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

About the Amphibian Conservation Fund

Amphibians are among the most threatened groups of vertebrates on Earth, facing growing pressures from habitat destruction, climate change, disease outbreaks, pollution, and invasive species.

The Amphibian Conservation Fund was established to provide financial support for projects that directly or indirectly improve the conservation status of threatened amphibian species.

The fund is held in trust and administered by Stiftung Artenschutz on behalf of a consortium of conservation partners, ensuring that resources are allocated to projects with strong scientific foundations and measurable conservation impact.

Funding Objective

The primary objective of the grant is to support initiatives that contribute to the long-term survival of threatened amphibian species.

Projects must clearly demonstrate how their activities will positively influence amphibian conservation and contribute to improved outcomes for species at risk.

Successful proposals should be evidence-based, practical, and aligned with internationally recognized conservation standards.

Eligible Project Activities

The Amphibian Conservation Fund supports a wide range of conservation initiatives, including:

Direct Amphibian Conservation

Projects that directly protect threatened amphibian populations through field conservation and species recovery activities.

Scientific Research

Research that generates critical knowledge to improve amphibian conservation, species management, and long-term survival.

Conservation Breeding

Establishment or expansion of ex-situ breeding programmes that help secure endangered amphibian populations.

Species Reintroduction

Carefully planned reintroduction programmes that restore threatened amphibian species to suitable natural habitats.

Habitat Restoration

Projects focused on restoring degraded ecosystems that are essential for amphibian survival.

Capacity Building

Training programmes that strengthen local conservation skills, institutional capacity, and technical expertise.

Education and Public Awareness

Community outreach, environmental education, and awareness campaigns that promote amphibian conservation and biodiversity protection.

Species Eligible for Funding

The grant primarily supports projects focusing on amphibian species classified on the IUCN Red List as:

  • Critically Endangered (CR)
  • Endangered (EN)
  • Vulnerable (VU)

However, proposals involving species classified as:

  • Data Deficient (DD)
  • Not Evaluated (NE)
  • Other conservation categories

may also be considered where there is a clearly demonstrated conservation concern or where surveys are needed to determine the species’ conservation status.

Conservation Standards

The Amphibian Conservation Fund follows internationally recognized conservation principles, including guidance developed by:

  • The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)
  • The IUCN Species Survival Commission (SSC)

Applicants are encouraged to design projects that reflect these internationally accepted conservation best practices.

Who Should Apply?

The grant is suitable for organizations and professionals working in biodiversity conservation, wildlife management, ecological research, and species recovery.

Potential applicants include:

  • Conservation NGOs
  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Wildlife conservation organizations
  • Zoological institutions
  • Amphibian specialists
  • Environmental organizations
  • Conservation practitioners
  • Breeding and reintroduction programmes

Collaborative projects involving multiple institutions are also encouraged where appropriate.

Supporting Organizations

The Amphibian Conservation Fund is supported by a network of respected conservation institutions and zoological organizations, including:

  • Aquazoo Düsseldorf
  • German Zoo Keeper Association (BdZ)
  • German Society for Herpetology and Herpetoculture (DGHT)
  • German Association of Animal Parks (DTG)
  • German Association of Wildlife Parks (DWV)
  • Tierpark Hellabrunn Munich
  • Association of Zoological Gardens (VdZ)
  • Association of German-speaking Zoo Educators (VZP)
  • Zoo Dresden
  • Zoo Heidelberg
  • Zoo Leipzig

Their collective commitment strengthens global efforts to protect amphibian biodiversity.

Why This Grant Matters

Amphibians play an essential role in maintaining healthy ecosystems by controlling insect populations, serving as indicators of environmental health, and contributing to ecological balance.

Yet they are experiencing one of the fastest rates of decline among vertebrate groups worldwide.

By funding innovative conservation projects, the Amphibian Conservation Fund helps safeguard biodiversity while supporting practical, science-based solutions that benefit both wildlife and local communities.

Apply Here 

Why You Should Apply

If your organization is working to conserve threatened amphibians through research, habitat restoration, conservation breeding, education, or species recovery, this grant offers valuable support to expand your impact.

Projects that demonstrate measurable conservation outcomes, strong scientific justification, and alignment with international conservation standards are well positioned for consideration.

For more information on this grant opportunity, visit here

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, here 

The post Amphibian Conservation Fund 2026 Grant Call Now Open | Funding Available for Global Amphibian Conservation Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa CDC Opens Applications for the Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC) 2026–2028
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) is inviting Expressions of Interest (EOIs) from distinguished African leaders, technical experts, policymakers, advocates, and public health professionals to serve on the newly established Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC). The Council is a high-level strategic advisory platform that will guide and accelerate the implementation of […]

The post Africa CDC Opens Applications for the Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC) 2026–2028 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) is inviting Expressions of Interest (EOIs) from distinguished African leaders, technical experts, policymakers, advocates, and public health professionals to serve on the newly established Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC).

The Council is a high-level strategic advisory platform that will guide and accelerate the implementation of the Africa CDC Continental Immunization Strategy (CIS) 2026–2030, helping strengthen immunization systems, improve health security, and expand equitable access to life-saving vaccines across the African continent.

Individuals with demonstrated leadership, technical expertise, and a strong commitment to advancing immunization in Africa are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 21 July 2026

About the Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC)

The Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC) is a strategic, action-oriented body established by Africa CDC to provide independent, high-level guidance on continental immunization priorities.

The Council will support the implementation of the Continental Immunization Strategy (2026–2030), which recognizes immunization as a cornerstone of:

  • Strong primary health care systems
  • Epidemic preparedness
  • Health security
  • Health equity
  • Sustainable development
  • Human capital development

By bringing together influential leaders from across Africa, the Council aims to strengthen political commitment, improve financing, foster accountability, and accelerate immunization coverage throughout the continent.

Role of CIAC Members

Members will serve as senior strategic advisors and advocates, helping Africa CDC identify opportunities, overcome challenges, and promote sustainable solutions for immunization systems.

Council members will:

  • Provide strategic advice on immunization priorities across Africa.
  • Identify emerging opportunities, risks, and implementation bottlenecks.
  • Champion immunization within primary health care and health security agendas.
  • Advocate for sustainable domestic financing for immunization programmes.
  • Promote stronger coordination among governments and development partners.
  • Participate in policy dialogues and technical consultations.
  • Join targeted working groups when needed.
  • Support accountability by reviewing progress and recommending actions.
  • Use their professional influence and networks to advance continental immunization priorities.

Expertise Africa CDC Is Looking For

Africa CDC seeks a diverse and balanced Council composed of leaders with recognized expertise and influence in areas critical to improving immunization outcomes.

Ideal candidates may have experience in:

Political and Policy Leadership

  • Government leadership
  • Parliamentary affairs
  • Diplomacy
  • Public policy development

Public Health Leadership

  • Immunization programmes
  • Primary health care
  • Health security
  • Health systems strengthening

Technical Immunization Expertise

  • Vaccine-preventable diseases
  • Immunization policy
  • Zero-dose and under-immunized populations
  • Outbreak prevention and response
  • Life-course vaccination

Health Financing

  • Public finance
  • Domestic resource mobilization
  • Health budgeting
  • Innovative financing
  • Investment case development
  • Sustainable financing for health

Community Engagement

  • Civil society leadership
  • Community mobilization
  • Risk communication
  • Demand generation
  • Youth engagement
  • Gender equity
  • Reaching underserved populations

Implementation Experience

Candidates with experience working in:

  • Fragile settings
  • Conflict-affected regions
  • Humanitarian contexts
  • Cross-border health programmes
  • Urban poor communities
  • Remote and hard-to-reach populations

are particularly encouraged to apply.

Membership Term

Selected members will serve:

  • Initial term: Two years (2026–2028)
  • Eligible for one additional two-year renewal based on:
    • Continued relevance of expertise
    • Active participation
    • Commitment to the Council’s work
    • Need for continuity and balanced membership

Expected Time Commitment

The Council has been designed to accommodate busy senior leaders while ensuring meaningful engagement.

Members should expect to contribute approximately three to five days each year, including:

  • Two formal Council meetings annually:
    • One virtual meeting
    • One in-person meeting
  • Reviewing background materials
  • Providing written or verbal advice
  • Participating in occasional consultations or working groups

The in-person meeting may be held alongside the Continental Immunization Forum or another major continental event.

What Members Will Contribute

CIAC members will help shape Africa’s immunization agenda by contributing:

  • Strategic guidance on continental priorities
  • Leadership in solving implementation challenges
  • Advocacy to keep immunization high on political agendas
  • Recommendations on sustainable financing
  • Advice on reaching zero-dose and under-immunized children
  • Support for integrating immunization into primary health care
  • Oversight through progress reviews and accountability mechanisms
  • Strategic influence through professional and institutional networks

Application Requirements

Interested individuals should submit an Expression of Interest package containing:

  • A brief biography
  • A letter of interest describing:
    • Relevant experience
    • Professional expertise
    • Motivation for serving on the Council
    • Specific contributions they would make to advancing immunization across Africa

Africa CDC emphasizes that applicants should possess a strong sense of urgency and commitment to improving immunization outcomes on the continent.

Selection Process

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Leadership profile
  • Technical expertise
  • Professional experience
  • Regional representation
  • Gender balance
  • Overall composition needed for an effective and representative Council

Africa CDC aims to establish a diverse, influential, and action-oriented advisory body capable of advancing immunization across all regions of Africa.

Why You Should Apply

Serving on the Continental Immunization Advisory Council offers an opportunity to influence the future of immunization policy across Africa.

Members will help shape continental strategies, strengthen health systems, improve vaccine equity, and contribute to protecting millions of lives through stronger immunization programmes.

If you are an experienced leader or expert committed to advancing public health and immunization in Africa, this is an opportunity to make a lasting impact at the continental level.

Apply Here 

For more information on this opportunity, visit here

Discover more global opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Africa CDC Opens Applications for the Continental Immunization Advisory Council (CIAC) 2026–2028 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WFD Zambia Representative Consultancy 2026–2027: Democracy and Governance Opportunity in Lusaka
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Westminster Foundation for Democracy Opens Consultancy Opportunity in Zambia The Westminster Foundation for Democracy (WFD), the United Kingdom’s leading organisation dedicated to strengthening democracy and governance globally, is seeking an individual consultant or organisation to represent WFD in Zambia. This consultancy presents an exciting opportunity for governance professionals, democracy experts, policy specialists, parliamentary strengthening practitioners, […]

The post WFD Zambia Representative Consultancy 2026–2027: Democracy and Governance Opportunity in Lusaka appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Westminster Foundation for Democracy Opens Consultancy Opportunity in Zambia

The Westminster Foundation for Democracy (WFD), the United Kingdom’s leading organisation dedicated to strengthening democracy and governance globally, is seeking an individual consultant or organisation to represent WFD in Zambia.

This consultancy presents an exciting opportunity for governance professionals, democracy experts, policy specialists, parliamentary strengthening practitioners, and organisations with strong networks across Zambia’s governance ecosystem to contribute to democratic development and institutional strengthening in the country.

The selected representative will work closely with WFD’s Africa team to explore strategic opportunities for WFD’s long-term engagement in Zambia. This includes partnership development, stakeholder engagement, programme identification, fundraising exploration, and supporting the organisation’s future operational presence in the country.

Applications close on 17 July 2026.

About Westminster Foundation for Democracy (WFD)

The Westminster Foundation for Democracy is the UK’s premier democracy-support organisation, working internationally to strengthen democratic institutions, accountability systems, policymaking processes, citizen participation, and inclusive governance.

WFD collaborates with parliaments, governments, political parties, civil society organisations, election management bodies, and development actors to support democratic reforms and improve governance outcomes.

Across Africa, WFD has delivered governance and democracy programmes since 2001, helping countries strengthen political participation, transparency, inclusion, public accountability, and institutional effectiveness.

WFD’s Previous Work in Zambia

WFD has already established a strong track record of engagement in Zambia through several impactful governance initiatives and partnerships.

Some of WFD’s recent work in Zambia includes:

  1. Roadmap of Electoral and Related Reforms (2022)
    • Supporting discussions and policy engagement around electoral reform processes.
  2. First-Time Voters Project (2022)
    • Delivered in partnership with the London School of Economics (LSE) to encourage youth participation in democratic processes.
  3. Zambian Parliamentary Caucus on Environment and Climate Change (2023–2024)
    • Supporting environmental governance and climate-related parliamentary initiatives.
  4. Environmental Democracy Study on Climate Change Adaptation Citizen Participation (2025)
    • Examining public participation and democratic engagement in climate adaptation policies.
  5. Costs of Politics in Zambia Report (2023)
    • Research focused on the financial barriers and political participation challenges in Zambia.
  6. Parliamentary Caucus on Post-Legislative Scrutiny (2024)
    • Supporting scrutiny of Zambia’s Food Reserve Act (2020).
  7. Support to Joint Political Party Caucus on Electoral Reforms (2026)
    • Facilitating collaborative engagement around electoral reform proposals.

Building on these initiatives, WFD now seeks to establish a stronger and more sustainable operational presence in Zambia.

WFD Representative Zambia Consultancy: Main Purpose of the Role

The selected consultant or organisation will support WFD in identifying strategic entry points where the organisation can add value to Zambia’s democracy and governance landscape.

The role involves:

  • Identifying new programme opportunities
  • Developing partnerships
  • Raising WFD’s visibility in Zambia
  • Engaging governance stakeholders
  • Exploring donor and funding opportunities
  • Advising on legal and operational requirements for establishing a local presence

The consultancy combines strategic analysis, stakeholder engagement, programme development, and representation responsibilities.

Location and Contract Details

Consultancy Location

  • Lusaka, Zambia

Contract Duration

  • August 2026 – March 2027
  • Possibility of extension depending on:
    • Progress achieved
    • Funding availability
    • Performance outcomes

Time Commitment

  • Approximately 50–80 hours per month

Remuneration

  • Estimated between:
    • £1,100 and £1,600 per month

Contract Type

  • Consultancy / Service Contract
  • Open to:
    • Individual consultants
    • Organisations

Key Responsibilities of the WFD Zambia Representative

The successful candidate will work closely with WFD’s UK and Africa teams to deliver a broad range of governance and programme-development activities.

Core responsibilities include:

1. Identifying Programme Opportunities

The representative will identify areas where WFD can support democratic governance, inclusion, transparency, and accountability in Zambia.

This includes:

  • Governance reform opportunities
  • Parliamentary strengthening initiatives
  • Civic participation projects
  • Electoral reform engagement
  • Public policy initiatives
  • Political inclusion programmes

2. Stakeholder Engagement and Networking

The consultant will build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders across Zambia’s governance ecosystem.

Stakeholders may include:

  • Government institutions
  • Parliamentarians
  • Political parties
  • Civil society organisations
  • Election management bodies
  • International donors
  • Development agencies
  • Research institutions
  • Governance networks

3. Raising WFD’s Visibility in Zambia

The role requires representing WFD at relevant governance events and helping increase awareness of WFD’s expertise and mission.

Activities may include:

  • Organising governance events
  • Hosting policy dialogues
  • Facilitating stakeholder meetings
  • Showcasing WFD research
  • Convening thematic discussions
  • Supporting governance-focused collaboration

4. Exploring Funding and Partnership Opportunities

The consultant will map potential donors and funding opportunities relevant to governance and democracy programming in Zambia.

This includes:

  • Identifying local and international funding agencies
  • Tracking governance funding priorities
  • Exploring partnership opportunities
  • Supporting future programme proposals

5. Advising on WFD Country Presence

The representative will provide strategic advice regarding WFD’s potential operational establishment in Zambia.

Areas of assessment include:

  • Registration options
  • Legal implications
  • Tax considerations
  • Hosting arrangements
  • Operational models
  • Administrative requirements

Expected Deliverables

WFD expects several major outputs from the consultant during the contract period.

Deliverables Include:

1. Stakeholder Mapping

Comprehensive mapping of:

  • Parliament
  • Government institutions
  • Political parties
  • Civil society organisations
  • Governance actors
  • Donor agencies
  • Potential implementing partners

2. Opportunity Assessment

Identification of:

  • 3–5 priority thematic areas for WFD engagement
  • Governance gaps and challenges
  • Potential programme concepts
  • Risk analysis
  • Alignment with donor priorities

3. Partnership Development

Evidence of engagement with:

  • 15–20 stakeholders
  • Funding organisations
  • Governance actors
  • Local implementing partners

4. Funding Pipeline Assessment

Development of:

  • Donor landscape analysis
  • Governance funding trends
  • Upcoming funding opportunities
  • Local funding prospects

5. Country Presence Assessment

Recommendations covering:

  • Registration structures
  • Legal and tax implications
  • Hosting arrangements
  • Operational options

6. Zambia Engagement Strategy (2027–2029)

A long-term strategic engagement roadmap for WFD’s future work in Zambia.

Qualifications and Experience Required

WFD is looking for highly experienced governance professionals or organisations with strong sector credibility and established networks.

Applicants should demonstrate:

Required Experience

  • Approximately 10+ years of professional experience in:
    • Democracy
    • Governance
    • Public policy
    • Political participation
    • Parliamentary strengthening
    • Accountability
    • Human rights
    • Inclusion
  • Strong experience engaging with:
    • Government officials
    • Parliamentarians
    • Political parties
    • Civil society organisations
    • Development partners
  • Existing professional networks across Zambia’s governance sector
  • Experience working with:
    • International organisations
    • Donors
    • INGOs
    • Multilateral institutions

Required Skills

Applicants should possess:

  • Excellent communication and representation skills
  • Strong stakeholder engagement abilities
  • Excellent English writing and reporting skills
  • High-level interpersonal and diplomatic skills
  • Ability to work independently
  • Strong organisational and strategic thinking abilities

Applicants must also:

  • Demonstrate political neutrality
  • Align with WFD’s democratic values
  • Have legal status and the right to work in Zambia

Conflict of Interest Disclosure

Applicants must disclose:

  • Political party positions
  • Elected office roles
  • Any interests that could create actual or perceived conflicts of interest with WFD’s work

What to Include in the Cover Letter

Applicants should explain:

  • Relevant experience connected to the Terms of Reference
  • How they intend to organise and manage the consultancy workload
  • How the consultancy fits alongside current commitments
  • Proposed remuneration:
    • Hourly
    • Monthly
    • Or full 8-month contract rate

Important Operational Information

The selected consultant or organisation will be responsible for:

  • IT equipment
  • Communication expenses
  • Tax obligations
  • Social security responsibilities
  • Local travel within Lusaka
  • Administrative costs

WFD notes that this initiative operates with modest resources and seeks the best-qualified applicant at reasonable cost.

Deadline for Applications

Application Deadline: 17 July 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early to avoid missing the deadline.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This consultancy offers governance professionals a unique opportunity to contribute to democratic development and institutional strengthening in Zambia while working alongside an internationally recognised democracy-support organisation.

It is particularly valuable for professionals seeking:

  • International governance consultancy experience
  • Strategic programme development work
  • High-level stakeholder engagement opportunities
  • Democracy and policy sector leadership exposure
  • Long-term development partnership opportunities

Professionals and organisations with strong governance expertise and established Zambia networks are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit:

Required Documents

  1. Cover Letter
  2. CV (in English)

Applications should be sent via email to:
Recruitment@wfd.org

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post WFD Zambia Representative Consultancy 2026–2027: Democracy and Governance Opportunity in Lusaka appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya Cohort 9 Applications Now Open | Win Up to $10,000 in Seed Funding
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for Cohort 9 of the Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya, an exciting programme designed to help women-led, tech-enabled startups grow, scale, and create lasting impact across Kenya. Funded by the Standard Chartered Foundation and implemented by Village Capital in partnership with the @iBizAfrica Incubation Centre at Strathmore University, the accelerator provides […]

The post Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya Cohort 9 Applications Now Open | Win Up to $10,000 in Seed Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for Cohort 9 of the Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya, an exciting programme designed to help women-led, tech-enabled startups grow, scale, and create lasting impact across Kenya.

Funded by the Standard Chartered Foundation and implemented by Village Capital in partnership with the @iBizAfrica Incubation Centre at Strathmore University, the accelerator provides ambitious women entrepreneurs with world-class business support, expert mentorship, networking opportunities, and access to catalytic funding.

The programme is committed to strengthening Kenya’s entrepreneurial ecosystem by helping women founders build resilient businesses, create employment opportunities, and drive technology-driven innovation.

Applications Are Now Open

Entrepreneurs are encouraged to apply for Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya – Cohort 9 and take advantage of a comprehensive startup support programme designed to accelerate business growth.

About the Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya

The Women in Tech Accelerator is a flagship entrepreneurship programme that empowers women founders by providing the resources, knowledge, and networks needed to scale successful businesses.

Recognizing the growing role of women entrepreneurs in Kenya’s innovation ecosystem, the accelerator supports founders who are building technology-enabled solutions that address real-world challenges while generating economic and social impact.

Through a structured incubation journey, participants receive practical support tailored to the specific needs of their businesses.

Programme Objectives

The accelerator seeks to:

  • Support women-led technology ventures.
  • Strengthen startup growth and sustainability.
  • Equip founders with practical business skills.
  • Expand access to mentorship and coaching.
  • Increase access to investment opportunities.
  • Foster innovation across Kenya.
  • Promote job creation through entrepreneurship.
  • Build a stronger ecosystem for women founders.

What Participants Will Receive

Selected startups will benefit from a range of growth-focused opportunities designed to help them scale their businesses.

1. Immersive Business Learning

Entrepreneurs will undergo a comprehensive assessment of their businesses to identify strengths, opportunities, and areas for improvement.

This evaluation helps shape a customized learning experience tailored to each venture’s unique stage of growth.

The programme delivers practical business training covering key aspects of startup development, enabling participants to strengthen their operations and prepare for scaling.

2. Expert Mentorship and Coaching

Participants will receive personalized guidance from experienced mentors and in-house coaches throughout the incubation programme.

Mentorship sessions are customized to address each company’s specific challenges and growth objectives.

Founders will benefit from expert advice on topics such as:

  • Business strategy
  • Leadership
  • Product development
  • Customer acquisition
  • Market expansion
  • Financial management
  • Investment readiness
  • Operational growth

3. Access to Seed Funding

One of the programme’s biggest highlights is the opportunity to secure funding.

The top seven startups selected during the accelerator will each have the opportunity to receive US$10,000 in seed funding to support business expansion and scaling.

The funding is intended to help founders accelerate growth, strengthen operations, and increase their impact.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is ideal for:

  • Women founders.
  • Women-led startups.
  • Technology-enabled businesses.
  • Innovative startups seeking growth support.
  • Entrepreneurs looking for mentorship and investment readiness.
  • Businesses with scalable solutions addressing market needs.

Whether your startup is focused on healthcare, education, agriculture, financial services, logistics, artificial intelligence, climate innovation, or other technology-driven sectors, the accelerator provides valuable resources to support your growth journey.

Programme Partners

The Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya is made possible through collaboration between leading organizations committed to entrepreneurship and women’s economic empowerment.

Standard Chartered Foundation

The programme is funded by the Standard Chartered Foundation, which supports initiatives that promote inclusive entrepreneurship and sustainable economic opportunities.

Village Capital

Village Capital is a global organization dedicated to supporting impact-driven entrepreneurs through investment readiness programmes, accelerator initiatives, and ecosystem development.

@iBizAfrica Incubation Centre

The accelerator is implemented alongside the @iBizAfrica Incubation Centre at Strathmore University, one of Kenya’s leading innovation and entrepreneurship hubs.

Why Join the Accelerator?

Participating startups will gain much more than funding.

Benefits include:

  • Tailored business training.
  • Personalized mentorship.
  • Professional coaching.
  • Business diagnostics.
  • Peer learning opportunities.
  • Networking with industry experts.
  • Increased investment readiness.
  • Opportunity to access seed funding.
  • Enhanced business resilience.
  • Greater visibility within Kenya’s startup ecosystem.

Building Kenya’s Innovation Ecosystem

The Women in Tech Accelerator continues to support women entrepreneurs who are transforming industries through innovation.

Previous cohorts have showcased inspiring businesses addressing challenges in sectors such as:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Maternal Health
  • Transportation
  • Digital Services
  • Education Technology
  • Financial Technology

The programme has become an important platform for helping women-led startups grow while contributing to Kenya’s economic development and job creation.

Apply Here

Why You Should Apply

If you are leading a tech-enabled startup and are ready to take your business to the next level, the Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya offers an exceptional opportunity to access world-class training, experienced mentors, valuable business networks, and potential seed funding.

With personalized support and exposure to leading entrepreneurship experts, participants will be better equipped to build sustainable businesses capable of creating meaningful social and economic impact across Kenya.

Apply for Cohort 9 and join a growing community of women innovators shaping the future of entrepreneurship in Africa.

For more information on this grant opportunity, visit here .

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, click here .

The post Women in Tech Accelerator Kenya Cohort 9 Applications Now Open | Win Up to $10,000 in Seed Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Social Sciences Postdoctoral & Fellowship Opportunities 2026: Top Fully Funded and High-Impact Research Positions Across Europe and the UK
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The academic and research landscape in 2026 continues to offer outstanding opportunities for early-career scholars in the social sciences, particularly in fields such as sociology, political science, anthropology, economics, gender studies, migration studies, artificial intelligence ethics, and public health. This comprehensive article highlights a curated list of active postdoctoral and fellowship opportunities across the United […]

The post Global Social Sciences Postdoctoral & Fellowship Opportunities 2026: Top Fully Funded and High-Impact Research Positions Across Europe and the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The academic and research landscape in 2026 continues to offer outstanding opportunities for early-career scholars in the social sciences, particularly in fields such as sociology, political science, anthropology, economics, gender studies, migration studies, artificial intelligence ethics, and public health.

This comprehensive article highlights a curated list of active postdoctoral and fellowship opportunities across the United Kingdom, Switzerland, Sweden, Austria, and broader Europe. These positions are ideal for researchers aiming to build international academic careers, contribute to cutting-edge research, and collaborate with world-leading institutions.

All opportunities listed below are currently open, with application deadlines extending into 2026 or operating on a rolling basis.

Postdoctoral Fellowship – Gender Studies (Sweden)

Karlstad University is recruiting a postdoctoral researcher in gender studies, focusing on gender equality, identity, and social transformation.

Key features:

  • Research on gender, society, and policy
  • Strong academic collaboration opportunities
  • Contribution to feminist and social theory research

Application:

  • Deadline: 15 August 2026
  • Apply here

 

Postdoctoral Position – Sociology of Health & STS (Switzerland)

SUPSI (University of Applied Sciences and Arts of Southern Switzerland) offers a postdoctoral position in sociology of health and science and technology studies (STS).

Key features:

  • Focus on health systems and science-society relations
  • Applied social science research environment
  • Strong interdisciplinary collaboration

Application:

  • Deadline: 23 August 2026
  • Apply here

 

Postdoctoral Fellowship – Migration Studies (ERC Project, Austria)

The University for Continuing Education Krems is hiring a postdoctoral researcher in migration studies, funded under an ERC research project.

Key features:

  • Research on migration, mobility, and integration
  • Participation in a European Research Council (ERC) project
  • Strong international research collaboration

Application:

  • Deadline: 24 August 2026
  • Apply here

 

Post-Doctoral Programme – Social Sciences (European Scientific Institute)

This program offers structured postdoctoral training and research opportunities across multiple social science disciplines.

Key features:

  • Interdisciplinary social science research program
  • Academic mentorship and career development
  • International collaboration opportunities

Application:

  • Deadline: 5 September 2026
  • Apply here

 

MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 (European Union)

The Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships are one of the largest research funding schemes in Europe.

Key features:

  • €399 million funding pool
  • Approximately 1,600 fellowships awarded
  • Mobility across EU and associated countries
  • Support for innovative research projects across disciplines

Application:

  • Deadline: 9 September 2026
  • Apply here

 

Postdoctoral Researcher – Social Science, AI & Ethics (Luxembourg)

The University of Luxembourg offers a rolling postdoctoral position focusing on the intersection of social sciences, artificial intelligence, and ethics.

Key features:

  • Research on AI governance and ethics
  • Interdisciplinary academic environment
  • Rolling applications accepted year-round

Application:

  • Deadline: Rolling
  • Apply here

 

Summary of Opportunities

These fellowships and postdoctoral positions provide:

  • Access to world-class research institutions across Europe and the UK
  • Funding for independent academic research
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Strong career pathways into academia, policy research, and international organizations
  • Exposure to global research networks in social sciences

 

Application Strategy Tips

To increase your chances of success:

  • Tailor your research proposal to each institution’s focus area
  • Highlight interdisciplinary experience
  • Demonstrate strong publication or research potential
  • Align your project with European research priorities (migration, inequality, AI ethics, health, gender studies)
  • Secure strong academic references early

 

 Learn More

  • More global opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Global Social Sciences Postdoctoral & Fellowship Opportunities 2026: Top Fully Funded and High-Impact Research Positions Across Europe and the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026: Google Opens Applications for African AI Startups and Researchers to Access Early Google DeepMind Models, Technical Mentorship, and Potential Funding
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries across the globe, and Africa is increasingly emerging as a hub for AI-driven innovation. To support the continent’s next generation of technology pioneers, Google has officially launched applications for the Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026—a groundbreaking initiative designed to accelerate African AI startups and researchers building solutions for […]

The post Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026: Google Opens Applications for African AI Startups and Researchers to Access Early Google DeepMind Models, Technical Mentorship, and Potential Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries across the globe, and Africa is increasingly emerging as a hub for AI-driven innovation. To support the continent’s next generation of technology pioneers, Google has officially launched applications for the Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026—a groundbreaking initiative designed to accelerate African AI startups and researchers building solutions for uniquely African challenges.

Hosted at the Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) in Ghana, the Google Africa Applied AI Lab provides founders and researchers with exclusive early access to Google DeepMind’s cutting-edge AI models, personalized technical mentorship from Google experts, commercialization support, venture capital connections, and opportunities for potential funding.

The initiative aims to bridge the gap between world-class AI research and commercially successful products, helping build Africa’s first generation of AI-native unicorn companies.

Applications are now open and will close on 31 August 2026.

What is the Google Africa Applied AI Lab?

The Google Africa Applied AI Lab is an innovation and commercialization platform established by Google Labs through the Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) in Ghana, with support from the Google AI Futures Fund.

Rather than functioning as a traditional accelerator or incubator, the programme focuses on collaborative product development between Google experts and selected African startups.

Its primary objective is to help founders transform promising AI ideas into scalable, market-ready products capable of solving significant challenges across Africa.

The programme connects entrepreneurs directly with:

  • Google Research experts
  • Google DeepMind engineers
  • Product specialists
  • Venture capital investors
  • AI researchers
  • Commercialization experts

Participants receive tailored technical support based on the maturity and needs of their startup.

The Mission of the Google Africa Applied AI Lab

The programme seeks to:

  • Accelerate AI innovation across Africa.
  • Support commercialization of breakthrough AI technologies.
  • Build globally competitive African AI startups.
  • Connect founders with world-class AI expertise.
  • Encourage responsible AI development.
  • Strengthen Africa’s AI ecosystem.
  • Foster collaboration between research and entrepreneurship.

Ultimately, the initiative aims to position Africa as a major contributor to the future of artificial intelligence.

Where is the Programme Based?

The Google Africa Applied AI Lab is headquartered at the:

Accra AI Community Centre (AICC)

Location:
Accra, Ghana

The AICC serves as one of Africa’s leading AI innovation hubs, providing collaborative workspaces, community programming, and technical support to entrepreneurs and researchers across the continent.

Although the programme culminates in an in-person event in Ghana, participants are not required to remain physically based at the Centre throughout the programme.

Focus Areas of the Google Africa Applied AI Lab

Google is looking for AI-powered products capable of addressing meaningful challenges across Africa.

The programme focuses on five major technological futures expected to shape the continent over the coming years.

1. The Future of Work

Innovations that improve productivity, employment, workforce development, automation, and digital labor.

Examples include:

  • Workforce AI tools
  • HR technology
  • Employment platforms
  • AI productivity assistants

2. The Future of Knowledge

Solutions that improve how people create, access, organize, and distribute knowledge.

Examples include:

  • Education technology
  • Knowledge management
  • AI tutoring systems
  • Information accessibility

3. The Future of Software Development

Products that enhance software engineering using AI.

Possible innovations include:

  • AI coding assistants
  • Development automation
  • Software testing
  • Programming productivity tools

4. The Future of Creativity

Projects supporting creative industries through AI.

Examples include:

  • Design tools
  • Creative assistants
  • AI-generated content
  • Digital art solutions

5. The Future of Entertainment

Applications using AI to transform media and entertainment.

Examples include:

  • Gaming
  • Interactive storytelling
  • Video creation
  • Music technologies

Benefits of the Google Africa Applied AI Lab

Selected startups gain access to a comprehensive package of technical, commercial, and investment support.

1. Early Access to Google DeepMind AI Models

Participants receive early access to Google’s newest AI technologies before they become publicly available.

Available models may include:

  • Gemini
  • Gemma
  • Veo

This gives startups a competitive advantage in developing innovative commercial AI products.

2. Technical Mentorship

Founders work closely with:

  • Google Research
  • Google engineering teams
  • Google AI specialists

Mentorship includes:

  • Product architecture
  • AI implementation
  • Technical guidance
  • Model optimization

3. Commercialization Support

The programme helps startups move from research and prototypes to scalable businesses.

Support includes:

  • Product validation
  • Market strategy
  • Business development
  • Go-to-market planning

4. Venture Capital Mentorship

Participants receive mentorship from leading venture capital firms, including:

  • 4DX Ventures
  • Norrsken22
  • Novastar Ventures
  • Ventures Platform

These firms provide guidance on:

  • Scaling startups
  • Fundraising
  • Investor readiness
  • Business strategy

5. Potential Funding Opportunities

Selected startups may also become eligible for investment opportunities through:

  • Google AI Futures Fund
  • Partner venture capital firms

Funding decisions are made separately and participation in the programme does not automatically guarantee investment.

6. Product Demo Day

The programme concludes with an in-person Demo Day at the Accra AI Community Centre.

Participants will present their AI products to:

  • Google teams
  • Investors
  • Venture capital firms
  • Strategic partners
  • Industry experts

This provides valuable visibility and networking opportunities.

7. No Equity Requirement

One of the programme’s most attractive features is that participating startups do not give up equity or ownership simply by joining the programme.

However, founders may later receive investment offers from Google AI Futures Fund or participating venture capital firms.

Programme Timeline

The Google Africa Applied AI Lab follows a structured timeline.

Applications Open

1 July 2026

Application Deadline

31 August 2026

Selection Notifications

September 2026

Co-development Period

Mid-September 2026 to Early December 2026

Demo Day

Early December 2026

Programme Structure

The programme consists of a collaborative co-development phase rather than classroom-based training.

Support is customized for each startup based on:

  • Product maturity
  • Technical needs
  • Commercial goals
  • Market readiness

The formal programme ends after Demo Day, but participants can continue accessing support through the Accra AI Community Centre.

Who Can Apply?

Google welcomes applications from individuals and teams building AI solutions for Africa.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Full-time startup founders
  • AI entrepreneurs
  • Researchers planning to become founders
  • AI innovators
  • Technology builders

Applicants should have ambitious ideas capable of generating significant impact across Africa.

Startup Stage Eligibility

The programme accepts startups across multiple stages, including:

  • Idea stage
  • Pre-Seed
  • Seed
  • Series A
  • Series B
  • Series C+

Although startups with commercial traction receive preference, applicants with promising ideas are also encouraged to apply.

Does Your Startup Need to Use Google AI?

Yes.

Applicants must demonstrate that Google AI technologies will play a meaningful role within their product.

However:

  • There is no exclusivity requirement.
  • Teams may continue using AI models from other providers alongside Google AI technologies.

Do You Need a Co-Founder?

No.

Solo founders are welcome to apply.

However, Google evaluates team composition as part of the selection process.

A well-balanced team may strengthen an application.

Do You Need to Be Technical?

Not necessarily.

Applicants do not need to be Google AI experts.

However, technical capability remains an important evaluation criterion because selected teams must successfully develop AI-powered products.

Selection Process

After applications close:

  1. Google reviews submissions.
  2. Applications are assessed against programme priorities.
  3. Additional information may be requested.
  4. Selected applicants are notified beginning in September 2026.

Applicants who are unsuccessful will be notified, although Google notes that it cannot provide individual feedback.

Venture Capital Partners

The programme collaborates with leading investment firms, including:

  • 4DX Ventures
  • Norrsken22
  • Novastar Ventures
  • Ventures Platform
  • Google AI Futures Fund

These organizations provide:

  • Mentorship
  • Strategic advice
  • Networking
  • Potential investment opportunities

About the Accra AI Community Centre

The Accra AI Community Centre (AICC) is an AI innovation hub located in Accra, Ghana.

Its mission is to:

  • Strengthen Africa’s AI ecosystem.
  • Support responsible AI innovation.
  • Reduce AI skills gaps.
  • Foster collaboration among entrepreneurs and researchers.
  • Provide a shared technology and innovation space.

The Google Africa Applied AI Lab operates from this centre.

About the Google AI Futures Fund

The Google AI Futures Fund (AIFF) supports innovators building the next generation of AI technologies.

The fund provides:

  • Capital
  • Early access to Google DeepMind models
  • Distribution opportunities
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Long-term support for breakthrough AI companies

Some participants in the Google Africa Applied AI Lab may become eligible for investment through AIFF.

Why You Should Apply

The Google Africa Applied AI Lab represents one of the continent’s most exciting AI commercialization opportunities.

Participants gain:

  • Early access to advanced Google AI models
  • Direct collaboration with Google experts
  • Personalized technical mentorship
  • Commercialization guidance
  • Venture capital exposure
  • Potential funding opportunities
  • Investor Demo Day
  • Access to Africa’s leading AI innovation hub
  • Continued support beyond the programme

Whether you are building AI for education, healthcare, finance, software, creativity, productivity, or entertainment, this initiative provides an exceptional platform to accelerate your startup.

How to Apply

Interested founders and researchers should submit their applications through the official Google Africa Applied AI Lab application portal.

Applicants should prepare:

  • Startup information
  • Product description
  • AI use case
  • Team details
  • Commercialization plan
  • Vision for impact across Africa

Applications must be submitted before the deadline.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

31 August 2026

Applications opened on 1 July 2026, and selected applicants will begin receiving notifications in September 2026.

Click HERE for more information about Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026.

Conclusion

The Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026 is a landmark initiative designed to empower Africa’s next generation of AI entrepreneurs and researchers. By combining Google DeepMind’s cutting-edge technologies with expert mentorship, commercialization support, venture capital access, and potential funding opportunities, the programme offers an unparalleled environment for building impactful AI solutions tailored to African realities.

Hosted at the Accra AI Community Centre in Ghana, the programme goes beyond traditional startup acceleration by fostering close collaboration between founders and Google experts while connecting participants with leading investors and strategic partners. For ambitious innovators seeking to transform groundbreaking ideas into scalable businesses capable of shaping Africa’s digital future, the Google Africa Applied AI Lab represents an exceptional opportunity. Interested founders and researchers are encouraged to prepare strong applications and submit them before the 31 August 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Fellowship, AI, and African opportunities on OFY.

The post Google Africa Applied AI Lab 2026: Google Opens Applications for African AI Startups and Researchers to Access Early Google DeepMind Models, Technical Mentorship, and Potential Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paid CRIS Research Internship 2026 in France: Apply for a 4-Month Research Internship at Sciences Po on Labor Market Inequalities
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
If you are a Master’s student interested in social science research, labor market inequalities, computational methods, data analysis, and programming, the Centre for Research on social InequalitieS (CRIS) at Sciences Po is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity beginning in September 2026. This four-month, part-time internship provides an excellent opportunity to work alongside experienced researchers […]

The post Paid CRIS Research Internship 2026 in France: Apply for a 4-Month Research Internship at Sciences Po on Labor Market Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a Master’s student interested in social science research, labor market inequalities, computational methods, data analysis, and programming, the Centre for Research on social InequalitieS (CRIS) at Sciences Po is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity beginning in September 2026.

This four-month, part-time internship provides an excellent opportunity to work alongside experienced researchers on a cutting-edge project that explores how skills influence labor market opportunities and inequalities. Interns will gain practical research experience, strengthen their analytical abilities, and contribute to a major academic research project using one of the largest datasets of online job advertisements.

The application deadline is 20 July 2026 at 9:00 AM CET.

About the Centre for Research on Social Inequalities (CRIS)

The Centre for Research on social InequalitieS (CRIS) is a joint research center established by Sciences Po and CNRS (French National Centre for Scientific Research).

CRIS is internationally recognized for producing high-quality, theory-driven empirical research on social inequalities. The center brings together:

  • 22 permanent researchers
  • 6 emeritus researchers
  • 29 PhD students
  • A dedicated six-member support team

Researchers at CRIS investigate inequality from multiple perspectives using interdisciplinary approaches and a wide variety of research methods, including:

  • Qualitative research
  • Quantitative research
  • Experimental methods
  • Comparative research
  • Micro-level analysis
  • Meso-level analysis
  • Macro-level analysis

Their research contributes to both academic knowledge and evidence-based policymaking.

Research Areas at CRIS

CRIS researchers specialize in numerous areas related to social inequalities, including:

  • Education
  • Labor market inequalities
  • Economic inequality
  • Social mobility
  • Welfare states
  • Public policy
  • Urban segregation
  • Migration
  • Discrimination
  • Social movements
  • Policing
  • Cultural consumption
  • Digital inequalities
  • Health inequalities
  • Environmental inequalities
  • Life course dynamics

The center emphasizes:

  • Empirical research
  • Methodological rigor
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • International perspectives
  • Comparative research
  • Theoretical pluralism

About the Internship Project

The internship forms part of an ongoing research project led by Marie Labussière.

The project seeks to better understand how workers’ skills influence labor market opportunities.

Although educational qualifications and occupations are commonly used to study labor markets, individual skills themselves have received comparatively less direct attention. This research aims to fill that gap by measuring skills directly through large-scale analysis of online job advertisements.

Using computational approaches and advanced data analysis techniques, the project investigates how different skills are valued across occupations and how they contribute to labor market inequalities.

What Will Interns Do?

Successful applicants will work closely with the project supervisor and contribute to several important research activities.

Primary responsibilities include:

  1. Conducting literature reviews on labor market inequalities and skills research.
  2. Analyzing large datasets of online job advertisements.
  3. Coding latent skill dimensions.
  4. Performing both manual coding and computational coding.
  5. Working with word embedding models.
  6. Supporting ongoing academic research activities.

Throughout the internship, participants will receive guidance from the project supervisor while developing valuable research and analytical skills in an academic environment.

Skills You Will Develop

This internship offers practical experience in several highly valuable areas, including:

  • Academic research
  • Literature review
  • Data analysis
  • Statistical analysis
  • Computational social science
  • Programming
  • Python applications
  • Word embedding models
  • Labor market research
  • Research methodology
  • Analytical thinking
  • Quantitative research methods

These skills are highly relevant for students considering careers in academia, research institutions, policy organizations, international organizations, or data-driven social science.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should meet the following requirements:

Educational Background

Applicants must be currently pursuing a Master’s degree in one of the following fields:

  • Sociology
  • Economics
  • Psychology
  • Computational Social Sciences

Technical Skills

Candidates should have:

  • Statistical knowledge
  • Experience with regression analysis
  • Basic knowledge of linear algebra, including vector projections
  • Programming proficiency

Knowledge of Python is considered an advantage. However, applicants with strong motivation to learn Python are also encouraged to apply.

Internship Duration

The internship will run for:

  • Duration: 4 months
  • Period: September to December 2026

Working Hours

The internship is part-time and requires:

  • 18 hours per week

This makes it suitable for students who wish to combine research experience with their academic commitments.

Internship Benefits

Successful interns will receive numerous professional and academic benefits, including:

  • Paid internship
  • Hands-on research experience
  • Mentorship from an experienced academic researcher
  • Experience working with large real-world datasets
  • Exposure to computational research methods
  • Development of statistical and programming skills
  • Opportunity to contribute to impactful research on labor market inequalities
  • Experience within an internationally respected research center
  • Strong addition to an academic CV

Compensation

Interns will receive:

  • Internship allowance at the legal minimum rate applicable under French law.

Internship Location

Administrative Address:

27 rue Saint Guillaume
 75337 Paris Cedex 07
 France

Office Location:

1 Place Saint Thomas d’Aquin
 75007 Paris
 France

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare:

  • A Letter of Intent
  • A Curriculum Vitae (maximum two pages)

Applications must be submitted through the official online application form.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 20 July 2026 at 9:00 AM CET

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official online application form and upload:

  • Letter of Intent
  • CV (maximum two pages)

Click here to apply

Click here for more information about this

Why You Should Apply

This internship presents an outstanding opportunity for Master’s students seeking practical research experience in computational social science and labor market analysis. Participants will collaborate with leading researchers, gain exposure to advanced analytical techniques, and contribute to an important project examining how skills shape labor market inequalities.

Whether you are planning a research career, pursuing a PhD, or looking to strengthen your analytical and programming skills, this internship offers valuable academic and professional experience within one of France’s leading research centers.

Click here for more global internship opportunities

 

The post Paid CRIS Research Internship 2026 in France: Apply for a 4-Month Research Internship at Sciences Po on Labor Market Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Free UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course 2026: Earn a UN Certificate and Learn How to Design Inclusive Training
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Creating training that truly reaches everyone requires more than good content—it requires intentional, inclusive design. If you’re a trainer, instructional designer, educator, project manager, or learning professional, the UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course offers a practical opportunity to build the knowledge and skills needed to make learning more accessible, equitable, and impactful. This free, self-paced […]

The post Free UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course 2026: Earn a UN Certificate and Learn How to Design Inclusive Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Creating training that truly reaches everyone requires more than good content—it requires intentional, inclusive design. If you’re a trainer, instructional designer, educator, project manager, or learning professional, the UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course offers a practical opportunity to build the knowledge and skills needed to make learning more accessible, equitable, and impactful.

This free, self-paced online course is open to learners from around the world and helps participants integrate gender equality, intercultural sensitivity, and disability inclusion throughout every stage of the training cycle. Upon successful completion, participants receive an official UNITAR Certificate of Completion.

More than 2,000 professionals from 154 countries have already completed the course this year, making it one of UNITAR’s widely accessed professional learning opportunities.

There is no application deadline, and registration is currently open.

About the Inclusive Learning Design Course

The Inclusive Learning Design Course is developed by the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) to help learning professionals design training that is accessible, inclusive, and aligned with international standards.

The course supports the implementation of the UN Disability Inclusion Strategy and promotes learning environments where every participant has an equal opportunity to access, engage with, and benefit from training.

Rather than treating inclusion as an afterthought, the course demonstrates how to incorporate inclusive principles into every phase of the learning process—from planning and design to delivery and evaluation.

What You Will Learn

Participants will gain practical knowledge and skills to:

  • Mainstream gender equality throughout training design and delivery.
  • Integrate disability inclusion into learning programmes.
  • Apply intercultural sensitivity across diverse learning environments.
  • Use inclusivity principles throughout the entire training cycle.
  • Design accessible learning materials.
  • Create inclusive learning environments.
  • Apply inclusive facilitation techniques.
  • Evaluate training programmes using inclusive approaches.
  • Apply the “Do No Harm” principle to avoid unintended negative consequences during training implementation.

Course Features

The course has been designed to provide a flexible and engaging learning experience.

Self-Paced Learning

Participants can complete the course entirely online at their own convenience.

  • Approximately 2 hours of learning time.
  • Study whenever your schedule allows.
  • No fixed class sessions.

Interactive Learning Experience

The course includes:

  • Real-world case studies.
  • Scenario-based learning activities.
  • Practical exercises.
  • Opportunities to apply inclusive design concepts immediately.

Official UNITAR Certificate

Participants who successfully complete the final assessment receive an official UNITAR Certificate of Completion, which can be added to professional portfolios, CVs, and LinkedIn profiles.

Practical Resources

Learners also gain access to downloadable resources, including:

  • Inclusivity checklists.
  • Job aids.
  • Practical guidance for implementing inclusive learning design.
  • Reference materials for future projects.

Who Should Take This Course?

This course is particularly valuable for professionals involved in designing, managing, or delivering learning programmes, including:

Learning Specialists and Instructional Designers

Learn how to integrate accessibility and inclusivity throughout the learning design process.

Trainers and Facilitators

Strengthen your facilitation techniques and learn how to create engaging, inclusive learning environments for diverse audiences.

Project Managers and Programme Officers

Ensure that training programmes align with quality standards, inclusion principles, and the UN Disability Inclusion Strategy.

The course is also suitable for:

  • Educators
  • Learning and Development professionals
  • NGO staff
  • Government officials
  • International development practitioners
  • Human resource professionals
  • Anyone interested in making learning more inclusive

Why Take This Course?

Inclusive learning benefits everyone. By participating in this course, you will:

  • Improve the quality of your training programmes.
  • Design learning experiences that are accessible to diverse audiences.
  • Strengthen your professional skills.
  • Gain internationally recognised knowledge from UNITAR.
  • Earn an official certificate at no cost.
  • Learn practical techniques that can be immediately applied in your workplace.
  • Contribute to more equitable and inclusive education and professional development.

Course Details

  • Course Type: Online
  • Cost: Free
  • Language: English
  • Learning Format: Self-paced
  • Duration: Approximately 2 hours
  • Certificate: Official UNITAR Certificate of Completion
  • Registration: Open

How to Enrol

Registration is currently open, and learners can begin immediately.

Start the course here

Why This Course Matters

As organisations increasingly prioritise accessibility, diversity, equity, and inclusion, professionals who understand inclusive learning design are becoming more valuable across sectors.

Whether you work in education, government, international development, humanitarian organisations, NGOs, or the private sector, this course provides practical skills that can help you create learning experiences that are more effective, equitable, and impactful.

Since there is no enrolment deadline, you can register today and begin learning immediately.

Click here for more global online courses

The post Free UNITAR Inclusive Learning Design Course 2026: Earn a UN Certificate and Learn How to Design Inclusive Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026: Up to $1.5 Million in Funding for High-Growth Small and Medium Enterprises Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are widely recognized as engines of economic growth, innovation, and job creation. However, many promising businesses in emerging markets struggle to access the financing and technical support needed to expand operations, enter new markets, adopt new technologies, and build long-term resilience. To help address these challenges, the SME Growth Accelerator […]

The post SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026: Up to $1.5 Million in Funding for High-Growth Small and Medium Enterprises Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are widely recognized as engines of economic growth, innovation, and job creation. However, many promising businesses in emerging markets struggle to access the financing and technical support needed to expand operations, enter new markets, adopt new technologies, and build long-term resilience.

To help address these challenges, the SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026 is now accepting applications. This funding opportunity provides between USD 50,000 and USD 1.5 million in grant funding, alongside mentorship, technical assistance, networking opportunities, and access to potential follow-on financing for high-potential SMEs operating across Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

The programme is specifically designed to support for-profit businesses with strong growth potential that are committed to creating quality jobs, strengthening local economies, and promoting sustainable and inclusive development.

Applications are currently open, and eligible SMEs are encouraged to submit their applications before the 15 November 2026 deadline.

About the SME Growth Accelerator Grant

The SME Growth Accelerator is a grant programme focused on supporting ambitious small and medium-sized enterprises operating in emerging markets.

The programme aims to help businesses overcome common barriers to growth by providing both financial support and strategic business assistance.

Rather than simply offering funding, the initiative combines capital with mentorship, technical support, and business networking to strengthen participating companies and improve their long-term competitiveness.

Successful applicants will gain access to resources that can help them:

  • Scale business operations
  • Expand into new domestic and international markets
  • Increase productivity
  • Strengthen organizational capacity
  • Improve sustainability practices
  • Create quality employment opportunities
  • Prepare for future investment opportunities

The programme recognizes that SMEs play a vital role in driving inclusive economic growth, innovation, and sustainable development across emerging economies.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive grant funding ranging from:

  • Minimum Funding: USD 50,000
  • Maximum Funding: USD 1.5 million

The amount awarded will depend on the proposed project, business needs, scalability, and expected development impact.

What the Grant Supports

The SME Growth Accelerator provides flexible funding across several business development areas.

Eligible projects may include the following:

1. Working Capital

Businesses can use funding to strengthen operational capacity and support day-to-day business activities that facilitate growth.

Examples include:

  • Business expansion
  • Operational scaling
  • Inventory financing
  • Growth initiatives
  • Pilot project implementation

2. Technical Assistance and Capacity Building

The programme supports initiatives that improve business performance through technical expertise and organizational development.

This may include:

  • Staff development
  • Business process improvement
  • Operational efficiency
  • Management systems
  • Organizational strengthening

3. Climate and ESG-Aligned Investments

The grant encourages investments that improve environmental sustainability and responsible business practices.

Examples include:

  • Climate-smart technologies
  • Sustainability initiatives
  • Environmental management systems
  • ESG implementation
  • Resource efficiency projects

4. Market Research and Feasibility Studies

Funding may also support businesses seeking to better understand new markets before expanding.

Examples include:

  • Market analysis
  • Customer research
  • Expansion feasibility studies
  • Product validation
  • Market entry planning

5. Equipment and Technology Procurement

Businesses can invest in equipment and technology that improve productivity and competitiveness.

Examples include:

  • Machinery
  • Digital technologies
  • Business software
  • Manufacturing equipment
  • Productivity tools

6. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning

Applicants may allocate part of the funding toward measuring project outcomes and strengthening organizational learning.

Activities may include:

  • Impact measurement
  • Data collection
  • Performance monitoring
  • Learning systems
  • Evaluation frameworks

Programme Benefits

The SME Growth Accelerator provides significantly more than financial support.

Successful applicants will benefit from several valuable opportunities designed to strengthen long-term business success.

These include:

Grant Funding

Businesses may receive between USD 50,000 and USD 1.5 million to implement approved growth projects.

Technical Assistance

Participants receive expert support that helps improve operations, strengthen internal capacity, and implement business growth strategies.

Mentorship

The programme connects participating SMEs with experienced mentors who provide strategic guidance throughout the growth journey.

Business Networks

Successful applicants gain access to valuable business networks that may create opportunities for:

  • Partnerships
  • Collaboration
  • Business development
  • Market expansion

Follow-On Financing Opportunities

High-performing businesses may also gain access to future financing opportunities that support continued expansion beyond the grant period.

Who Can Apply?

The SME Growth Accelerator is open to eligible small and medium-sized enterprises operating in emerging markets.

Applicants must meet all programme eligibility requirements.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must meet the following conditions.

Business Type

Applicants must be:

  • For-profit small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs)

Number of Employees

Eligible businesses must employ between:

  • 10 and 250 employees

Annual Revenue

Applicants must demonstrate verified annual revenue between:

  • USD 250,000 and USD 10 million

Geographic Eligibility

The programme is open to eligible SMEs operating within:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Latin America

Why SMEs Should Apply

Many businesses possess strong products and services but lack sufficient capital and strategic support to reach their full potential.

The SME Growth Accelerator addresses this challenge by combining financial resources with business development services that enable companies to grow sustainably.

Participating businesses can use the programme to:

  • Expand production
  • Enter new markets
  • Improve competitiveness
  • Build organizational capacity
  • Strengthen sustainability practices
  • Generate employment
  • Increase long-term resilience

For SMEs with ambitious growth plans, this programme offers an opportunity to accelerate business development while contributing to broader economic growth within their countries.

Application Process

The application process has been designed to be simple and efficient.

Applicants can complete the submission online.

According to the programme information:

  • The application takes approximately 15 minutes to complete.
  • There are no application fees.
  • Applications are reviewed according to established World Bank Group standards.
  • Applicants can expect a decision within 4 to 6 weeks after submission.

Interested businesses should ensure they prepare all required business information before beginning the application.

APPLY HERE

Important Dates

Applicants should note the following timeline:

  • Application Status: Open
  • Application Deadline: 15 November 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Why This Grant Matters

Access to growth capital remains one of the greatest barriers facing SMEs across emerging markets.

Without adequate financing, many businesses struggle to:

  • Expand production
  • Hire additional staff
  • Purchase equipment
  • Improve operational systems
  • Enter new markets
  • Adopt sustainable technologies

The SME Growth Accelerator directly addresses these challenges by combining grant funding with mentorship, technical assistance, and networking opportunities.

Its emphasis on sustainable growth, climate resilience, ESG alignment, and inclusive economic development makes it particularly valuable for businesses seeking long-term impact.

By supporting SMEs that demonstrate strong growth potential, the programme contributes to job creation, economic resilience, innovation, and sustainable private-sector development across Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

Tips for Preparing a Strong Application

To maximize the likelihood of success, applicants should:

  • Clearly explain their business model and growth strategy.
  • Demonstrate measurable business performance and verified annual revenue.
  • Present a realistic budget aligned with eligible funding activities.
  • Highlight expected social, economic, or environmental impact.
  • Show how grant funding will contribute to sustainable business growth.
  • Explain plans for job creation, innovation, or market expansion.
  • Submit the application well before the closing date.

Visit HERE to learn more about the SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026.

Conclusion

The SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for high-potential small and medium-sized enterprises across Africa, Asia, and Latin America to secure substantial funding and strategic business support. Offering grants ranging from USD 50,000 to USD 1.5 million, the programme goes beyond financial assistance by providing mentorship, technical assistance, access to professional networks, and opportunities for follow-on financing.

Whether your business is looking to expand into new markets, invest in technology, strengthen sustainability practices, or create quality jobs, this programme offers valuable resources to accelerate long-term growth.
